close_reason: fixed by .32 — zeroize now runs on all paths
description: In secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs put(), the line 'secret_bytes = value.as_bytes().to_vec()' creates a plain (non-Zeroizing) Vec<u8> holding secret material. The explicit zeroize() call at lines 458-462 only executes after create_object succeeds. On any earlier error path (session open failure, find_object failure, destroy failure when overwriting, or create_object failure), the closure returns early and 'template_create' — which contains the secret bytes as a CKA_VALUE Attribute — is dropped without zeroization. Fix: wrap secret_bytes in Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> so it is zeroized on drop regardless of which path the closure exits from.
title: pkcs11: secret bytes not zeroized on put() error paths
